passes.description: Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This pass is an e-voucher . Coupon is delivered by email. Coupon offers admission for up to 4 people (adults or children) at half price per person. All visitors after their first birthday require a ticket. After receiving a library discount coupon, the Boston Children’s Museum recommends reserving your entry ticket with them online as museum capacity is limited at this time. A library pass is not an admission ticket and does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum.

passes.description: The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Discount code is e-mailed to the patron. The discount code provides access to purchase tickets at 50% off the regular price for the following: Spectacle Island May 16 - June 15: Thursdays through Sundays June 16 - Sep 1: every day Sep 4 - 21: Thursdays through Sundays Sep 26 - Oct 13: Fridays through Sundays Georges Island June 16 - Sep 1: every day Spectacle Island June 16 - Sep 1: every day Sep 4 - Oct 13: Thursdays through Sundays Ferries to Georges and Spectacle Islands are boarded at Long Wharf North in Downtown Boston. Ferries to Peddocks Island are boarded from Hingham.

passes.description: Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon . Patrons must pick up their pass at the Library up to one week before their visit. It does not need to be returned to the library. Each pass admits up to 6 people to either farm park with a savings of $10 per person off the purchased-at-the-gate general admission rate. Children under 2 are always free. Davis Farmland Discovery Farm is open mid-April - October. Davis Mega Maze is open mid-September - October. Pass is not valid with other discounts, groups or special events. For the most up-to-date information on programs, hours, or closing due to weather, please visit the David Farmland website , or call (978) 422-MOOO (6666). Adults must be accompanied by a child 12-years of age and under.

passes.description: Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is emailed to the patron. Only Valid for the Reserved Date of Visit. Each coupon allows for admission of three people to the Museum at half price off regular admission. Children under age one are free. A library coupon is NOT a museum entry reservation. Advanced museum entry reservation is not available for Library Pass users. Present your Library Pass confirmation page to Admissions staff when checking in and the discount will be applied to the cost of your visit. Please consult the Plan Your Visit page on the Museum website for the most up-to-date policies, hours, and information.

passes.description: The EcoTarium is a unique indoor-outdoor museum in Worcester, MA. Set in an urban oasis, the museum offers a chance to walk through the treetops, take a multimedia journey through the galaxy at a digital planetarium, meet wildlife, stroll nature trails, and get hands-on with family-friendly exhibits.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each coupon admits up to 2 adults and 2 children in one vehicle at 50% off admission. Children under 2 are always free. When using the Library Pass discount, tickets cannot be reserved online - tickets must be purchased in person. These coupons do not cover the cost for tickets to the Alden Planetarium, the Explorer Express Train, or other special ticketed events. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit their website.

passes.museum_name: Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)
passes.description: The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: This is a SET OF 2 laminated passes, each pass offers discount to ONE person: Adults (age 18 and over) receive 50% the regular admission price. Children (age 6 to 17) are admitted for $1. Children (age 5 and younger) are admitted for free. Patrons may pick up their passes the day before or the day of their visit. The passes must be returned to the Wayland Library by the day after your visit . Tickets are available at the Visitor Center when you arrive. Please check their website for weather closings, special events, parking updates, etc. before visiting.

passes.description: Gore Place is a historic 19th century country house located in Waltham, MA. Indoor and outdoor tours of the 50-acre estate—which boasts a magnificent Mansion, an authentic 1793 Carriage House, a working farm, and beautiful grounds—are available; check the website for the schedule. Special events include an annual sheepshearing festival and a summer concert series. There is a museum shop on the premises.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Coupon is emailed to the patron. Only Valid for the Reserved Date of Visit. This coupon is good for: - Daytime Mansion Tour admission for two adults at $5 per person, and two children free (regularly $16 per adult and $11 for children ages 6-16). Mansion tours are offered Monday, Wednesday, Friday, and Saturdays. -One Take-a-Walk bag rental at $2/hr (regularly $5). Take-a-Walk bags for youth ages 4 and up contain outdoor seasonal activities for up to two children and are available Monday-Saturday. -Story Time for two children at $5 per child, and an accompanying adult free. Please note: The Mansion Tour coupon is good for daytime Mansion tours only and may not be used during evening or special themed tours or during special events such as the Sheep Shearing Festival at Gore Place. Days and times are subject to change, so it is best for visitors to consult the museum website when planning your visit. Event and holiday closings as well as school vacation hours are all listed on goreplace.org/whats-on .

passes.museum_name: Harvard Museums of Science and Culture
passes.description: The Harvard Museums of Science & Culture is a partnership of museums on the Harvard University Cambridge campus dedicated to the natural world, science, and human cultures. They include the Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments, the Harvard Museum of Natural History (comprised of the Harvard University Herbaria, Mineralogical and Geological Museum, and Museum of Comparative Zoology), the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East, and the Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, all located between Oxford St./Divinity Ave area. Of particular note are the famous glass flowers, models commissioned by Harvard in 1886 to aid in the teaching of botany (and made in Germany over the course of more than eight decades), on display in the Harvard Museum of Natural History.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each coupon will allow four patrons into the museums at a 50% off rate regardless of age / admission level. Please be prepared to show the physical library pass and proof of Massachusetts residency. Only one member of the party must be a MA resident. The pass allows admission to both the Harvard Museum of Natural History and Peabody Museum of Archaeology & Ethnology, as well as two free museums, the Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East (formerly the Harvard Semitic Museum) and the Harvard Collection of Historical Scientific Instruments. Patrons will enter through the Natural History or Peabody Museum with their hard copy pass. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit hmsc.harvard.edu .

passes.description: The building that houses the Institute of Contemporary Art/Boston on Fan Pier is the first in the US designed by the architectural firm of Diller Scofidio + Renfro. The museum was founded in 1936 as a sister institution to New York's MoMA, though it had no permanent collection until starting one after moving into the new building on the waterfront in 2006. In addition to the collection, changing exhibitions, music, dance, film, talks, tours, family activities, and teen programming are offered throughout the year.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is e-mailed to the patron. This library pass entitles you to up to two tickets for Gallery Admission at the discounted Library Pass price of $10 per ticket. Library Passes are not valid for performances, events, or films. This library pass is not a museum admission ticket. After reserving your library pass, follow the emailed instructions on how to reserve your free Gallery Admission tickets.

passes.description: The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Promo code and confirmation page are e-mailed to the patron. Each pass provides up to four (4) people with $5 admission per person. Children under the age of 18 are always admitted for free. You must print your library pass confirmation page or display it on your phone. The Museum requires also reserving timed-entry tickets in advance of your visit. PATRONS MUST PRESENT BOTH THE LIBRARY PASS AND THEIR RESERVED TIMED MUSEUM ADMISSION TICKETS FOR ENTRY INTO THE MUSEUM. A library pass is not an admission ticket and does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum.

passes.description: The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: No pass needed, just your library card! Present your physical or digital Massachusetts library card at the Admissions Desk to receive a 50% discount on one admission ticket. This offer is valid for visitors aged 13 and over and EACH VISITOR must present a Massachusetts library card to receive the discount. You must have your physical or digital library card in hand at the museum; the museum cannot access the library system to look up your account. A digital version of your library card can be accessed on your mobile device via the Minuteman Library Network app. You will need to know your account username or library card number, and your library account password or PIN. Your password can be reset at https://wayland.minlib.net/MyAccount/Home . Visitors aged 12 and under always receive free admission, with or without a library card. Please reach out to the JFK Library Visitor Services Department with questions at kennedy.groupvisits@nara.gov or 617-514-1600. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit their website, jfklibrary.org .

passes.description: Mass Audubon has over 55 wildlife sanctuaries and centers throughout Massachusetts where visitors can learn about and interact with nature. More than 38,000 acres of protected land deliver inspiring hilltop views to breathtaking coastal landscapes, serene woods, and working farms. Visit their website for a list of locations.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is emailed to the Patron; Print out the pass or display it on your phone at the Museum entrance. Each pass admits up to 4 people at $2 per person to any Massachusetts Audubon Wildlife Sanctuary, including Drumlin Farm in Lincoln, MA. (Children under age 2 are always free.) Holiday calendar, days open and hours of operation vary by property - please check the Mass Audubon website, massaudubon.org , for more details. Drumlin Farm Hours: Tuesday–Sunday, 9am-5pm • Closed Mondays (except holidays) • Closed on Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day. For the most up-to-date information on the Drumlin Farm programs and hours, please visit their website .

passes.museum_name: Massachusetts State Parks (Department of Conservation and Recreation)
passes.description: The Massachusetts State Parks have locations in all regions of the Commonwealth, from Pittsfield to Boston, and from Gloucester to Provincetown. They include beaches, wooded parks, parkways, and reservoirs, each with its own unique activities and amenities like hiking, biking, swimming and boating, camping, and more.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: This is a laminated pass that must be picked up and returned to Wayland. Patrons may pick up their pass during the Library's open hours on the day before or the day of their visit. The pass must be returned by the day after its use. Pass allows free daytime parking for one carload of visitors to all Massachusetts State Parks that charge a parking fee. (Except Nahant and Nantasket). See the Massachusetts Department of Conservation & Recreation website for the list of parks that charge a fee and the most up-to-date information on park programs and hours.

passes.museum_name: Massachusetts State Police Museum & Learning Center
passes.description: The Massachusetts State Police Museum and Learning Center is located on the site of an old state police barracks built in 1931 in in Grafton, MA. Visitors may explore the agency's past, as well as learn about current law enforcement methods and public safety efforts. Exhibits include a range of State Police-related items and historic memorabilia.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: This is a laminated pass that must be picked up and returned to Wayland. Patrons may pick up their pass during the Library's open hours on the day before or the day of their visit. The pass must be returned by the day after its use. Pass allows free admission for 2 people age 16+. Kids age 15 & under are always free. The Museum is open Tuesdays and Saturdays from 11am-4pm. Please see the Museum's website ( mspmlc.org ) for the most up-to-date information.

passes.description: The Boston Museum of Fine Arts, in existence since July 4, 1876, is one of the most comprehensive art museums in the world, with a collection of almost 500,000 works of art, ranging from ancient Egyptian artifacts to contemporary painting, sculpture and video. In addition, there are always special exhibitions on view, opportunities to take advantage of educational programs—some right in the galleries—and a contemplative Japanese garden.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Promo Code is e-mailed to the patron. Each Promo Code use allows 2 people at $15 per adult, $10 per child age 7-17. Children 6 and under are admitted for free. Note: The date of your museum visit must match the date on the library pass. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit their website .

passes.description: The Museum of Science in Boston is located on a piece of land that extends the width of the Charles River, connecting Boston and Cambridge. Dedicated to promoting and educating visitors about science and technology, the museum includes a planetarium, the world's largest open-air Van de Graaff generator, an IMAX theatre, a butterfly garden, and permanent and changing exhibits on birds, insects and animals; space; dinosaurs; engineering; nanotechnology; math; biology; and more.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is e-mailed to the patron. This pass offers up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are always admitted for free. A library pass is not a museum admission ticket. See emailed instructions for how to purchase your tickets at the discounted rate. For the most up-to-date information on museum programs and hours, please visit the museum's website.

passes.description: The New England Aquarium is home to thousands of aquatic animals, from the giant Pacific octopus to Northern fur seals to little blue penguins. They offer a wide range of exhibits, including a shark and ray touch tank and the Giant Ocean Tank, a four-story coral reef featuring hundreds of Caribbean animals. In addition, they have an IMAX theatre and run whale watch boat excursions in partnership with Boston Harbor Cruises from April through November.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is emailed to the patron. This coupon allows 50% off admission to the New England Aquarium for up to 4 people. This Library pass is not an Aquarium entry ticket. Redeem this coupon in the General Admission line at the Aquarium’s ticket booth for discounted admission. Advanced reservations are not available for library patrons with coupons. Please visit neaq.org/coupons for details. Review the Aquarium’s safety guidelines at neaq.org/safety ahead of your visit to ensure a safe and accessible experience. -Valid only for Aquarium admission. -This coupon does not provide any other discounts and cannot be applied to combo tickets. -Applies only to regularly priced Adult and Child tickets -Library coupons or tickets obtained with library coupons may not be sold. -Void if altered. No cash value. -Hours of operation are subject to change.

passes.museum_name: New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill
passes.description: Located in Boylston, MA, and open year-round, the New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill features carefully planned gardens and collections of ornamental, edible and native plants, with a number of trails that traverse the 200-acre property. In addition, there are many programs and events, including classes, lectures, art exhibits and concerts. The Garden is run by the Worcester County Horticultural Society, the third oldest active horticultural society in the United States.
passes.pass_type_raw: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
passes.benefits_text: This is a laminated pass that must be picked up and returned to Wayland. Patrons may pick up their pass during the Library's open hours on the day before or the day of their visit. The pass must be returned by the day after its use. Each pass offers 2 people half price general admission. Discount not valid for special events such as Night Lights. The physical pass from the library must be shown at check-in. The Library Discount Pass is not an entry ticket to the Garden. For the most up-to-date information on New England Botanic Garden at Tower Hill's programs and hours, please visit their website.

passes.museum_name: Trustees of Reservations (with deCordova Sculpture Park and Museum)
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each pass provides free or reduced admission for 2 adults and any number of children (under the age of 18) at 116 preserved lands and estates across Massachusetts, including Fruitlands and Old Manse. Discounts vary between properties. Please note, each pass is valid only toward general admission fees and not special events. The Library coupon is not an admission ticket. Follow emailed instructions to purchase tickets at the Library Discount rate. For more information, and for preserve locations, please visit the organization's website at www.thetrustees.org or call 978-921-1944. NOTE: deCordova is CLOSED Mondays & Tuesdays.

passes.address: Charlestown Navy Yard, Building 22, Charlestown, MA 02129
passes.description: The museum, a part of the Boston National Historical Park, is located in the Charlestown Navy Yard near the ship USS Constitution (“Old Ironsides”) at the end of Boston's Freedom Trail. The museum provides a detailed look at the history of the ship, information about the war of 1812, and the battle between the ship and HMS Java. Stories are told through interactive exhibits, unique artifacts, archival records, and artwork. The museum also includes a research library which houses a strong collection of manuscripts, books, artifacts, and microfilm of primary source material. The ship itself is maintained by the US Navy, and may be visited free of charge on certain days.
passes.pass_type_raw: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
passes.benefits_text: This is an e-voucher . Pass is e-mailed to the patron. This pass admits 5 people for free. You can either present a printed Library pass or display it from your phone or other device at the Museum. Valid only for the date listed on the pass. Limit one pass per party. Please note that the ship and museum have different hours. Museum Hours: Open Daily; Winter Hours: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Summer Hours: 9:00AM - 6:00PM. The Museum closes for Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day, and closes early on Christmas Eve.

passes.description: Zoo New England operates Franklin Park Zoo, a 72-acre site nestled in Boston's historic Franklin Park, and the Stone Zoo, located on a 26-acre site near Spot Pond reservoir in Stoneham, MA. In addition to animals, both zoos are engaged in wildlife and conservation programs, research, and education.
passes.pass_type_raw: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
passes.benefits_text: This is a paper coupon that must be picked up at the Wayland Library during the library's open hours no more than 1 week before its use date. It does not need to be returned. Each pass admits up to 6 persons at $9 per adult and $6 per child (ages 2-12). Children under 2 are admitted for free. The library discount coupon is not a Zoo admission ticket. Your coupon can be redeemed in person, and tickets can be purchased at the gate. Online reservations are not needed. Library discount passes may be used for general daytime admission only. They cannot be used for evening events such as Boston Lights. For the most up-to-date information on zoo programs and hours, please visit their website.
